Or in that sales process, that they might not have the funds, the money, the funding, or the budget to purchase what you're offering. Do you wanna keep wasting time? No, you don't, okay? So what do you do if you get a sense early in the call that they might not have the funding? So I'm gonna give you. Well, I said I was going to give you three questions on this. I think I'm going to give you about four or five, all right? So make sure you write these down. IG, make sure you write this down. TikTok, write this down. Facebook group, write this down.
LinkedIn, YouTube, Facebook page, people on my own Facebook, make sure you write this down if you want to sell more, okay? Here's rule number one. If you want to make hundreds of thousands of dollars a year as a sales professional, I'm not talking about a business owner. If you're a business owner and you want your teams to scale and do two to three times the sales they're doing now, or if you're an individual salesperson and you want to make hundreds of thousands of dollars a year-end commissions, you never waste your time with prospects who give you signals in the beginning. Of that conversation or sales process that they are completely broke, homeless, maybe their company's about to go bankrupt, whether you sell B2B or B2C. Never waste time with people like that.
You have to quickly financially qualify them. I see so many freaking salespeople in every industry we're in that waste so much time. Much of their precious selling time with prospects that can never ever get the funds or the money to buy what they offer. It takes away so much of your time being with prospects who can't get their funds together. You are literally doing free consulting. Stop the madness, okay? You got to stop, all right? Let me give you an example. Let's say you sold, in this example, I'm going to give you an industry-specific example so you can see how the process works. Let's say that you sold real estate training. Let's say you sold real estate training, train a lot of companies in that as well.
You teach people, you have some type of course you guys have seen them all online, you've seen them on TV, and let's say that your real estate course teaches people how to become full-time real estate investors, okay? So let's say, in the beginning, they give you a thing like, 'Oh gosh, it's just so stressful living paycheck to paycheck,' or... It's just been so stressed because you know our home got foreclosed on a month ago and we've just been you know we've been living with our aunt in the basement. Okay, those are financial red flags if you sell B2C. Now, if you sold B2B, financial flags might be, you know, we just went through a recent merger and our CEOs really cut our... budget by like 80%.
Well, that's a freaking financial flag if I'm selling B2B as well. Or, you know, the company is about to declare bankruptcy. You're selling to a smaller company. Yeah, we've had really struggled. Like we're thinking about filing bankruptcy. Well, that's a red flag possibly. Okay. I want to financially qualify them. It doesn't mean they can't get the funding. If they say they can, we'll continue. But if, like, there's no way that we can, we're out on the streets tomorrow. Do you want to waste the next 30 or 40 minutes? No. You want to move on to somebody else because you can't help them and you're just wasting their time too, okay? So if you find out early in that conversation that they might not have the funding, let's say you're selling B2C.
I'll give you an example. In this example, let's say you sell real estate education. You sell courses to people. That teaches them how to become full-time real estate investors, how to invest in flips and wholesale deals and all that kind of stuff. Airbnb, all that stuff. Okay, John, let's do this. So they're like, 'Yeah, Jeremy, just been really struggling, been living paycheck to paycheck.' And I said, 'Let's do this.' So just to, I guess, first and foremost. I need to make sure we can actually help you. I'm assuming you understand when you really get involved in real estate, when you have a real estate business. When you're wanting to start one, it's going to require capital or credit to put into the right training and systems so your business can actually make money, right?
So with your situation, from what you just said, what type of funding or what type of credit do you have access to or can get access to to put into a business so you can make more money? Because that's the problem that people that sell that solve, right? They teach them real estate so they can make more money. If they come back and say, 'Oh, I have no money. I have $100 or I have $500,' but your solution is $10,000. Well, that's going to be a problem, right? Okay, yeah, I don't have any money. I've got maybe a few hundred dollars to my name. Okay, well, with this business, just so you're aware, you're going to need to put in, you know, close to $10,000 when you start it.
To ensure you have the right training. To make sure you're profitable within the next 90 days. Is that something you can come up with if it fits into what you're looking for? Whatever your price point is. Now, if they say no, you can then give them options on how other people get the funding. So let me give you this example. There's different avenues you can take it. No, I don't really have the funding. I don't have it. Well, what I can do if it helps you is I can show you what are there are other clients do if they don't have the funds to start their business, to really invest in real estate. And you'll have to see if you can get your funds together.
You know, if you're wanting to leave your job and become a full-time investor like our clients are now doing. Now, notice how I tied in what? I positioned them getting the funding, the funds, the money together. I positioned them getting the money with what? To get what they said they wanted. If they can get the funds, they get what they want. If they can't get the funds, they don't get what they want. That's how you get prospects thinking results-based thinking over price or cost-based thinking. Did you see what I just did there? Okay. Then you're going to go over different options that members do to get the funding, lending from a bank, credit cards, home equity, 401k, whatever it is.
And at the end of that, you say, 'And what are those avenues do you have?' Now, if they still come back and say, 'Oh, let's say in this example, like, oh, yeah, I could get together maybe five, seven, eight grand, and you haven't told them the funding.' And let's say your company offers a payment plan, you could then say, 'Okay. Yeah. I just wanted to make sure that we could actually help because with what we do for our clients, you'll need to put in around five, you know, seven, maybe 9,000.' When you start to put into the training to make sure you're profitable in real estate starting out with us. Is that something you can come up with if it fits into what you're looking for?
